[[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Ancient Japanese Burial Mound | Name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | Location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | Image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial reference sites]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:丸山古墳]]

{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial reference sites]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:丸山古墳]]

These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial reference sites]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]]

[[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site | Name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial reference sites]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=丸山古墳 |別名=五条野丸山古墳/大軽丸山古墳/見瀬丸山古墳 |所在地=[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]見瀬町・五条野町・大軽町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 28|緯度秒 = 33.12 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 53.98 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[前方後円墳]]（剣菱型） |規模=墳丘長318m
高さ21m |築造年代=[[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]推定）[[天武天皇]]・[[持統天皇]]
（推定）[[欽明天皇]]・[[蘇我堅塩媛|堅塩媛]] |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「畝傍陵墓参考地」 |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「丸山古墳」 |出土品=[[須恵器]] |画像=[[Image:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{国土航空写真}} |特記事項=全国第6位/奈良県第1位の規模[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。
大王墓としては最後の前方後円墳 |地図 = Japan Nara |ラベル = 丸山古墳 |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北北西 }} '''丸山古墳'''（まるやまこふん、五条野丸山古墳/大軽丸山古墳/見瀬丸山古墳）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]見瀬町・五条野町・大軽町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、後円部墳頂は[[宮内庁]]により「'''畝傍陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第40代[[天武天皇]]・第41代[[持統天皇]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。また宮内庁治定部分を除く古墳全域は国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（指定名称は「丸山古墳」）。 奈良県では最大、全国では第6位の規模の巨大古墳で、大王墓としては最後の前方後円墳になる。 == 概要 == 「丸山」という名のとおり、以前よりこの古墳は単なる[[円墳]]とされてきたが、下記調査のとおり極めて大規模な[[前方後円墳]]である。築造は[[6世紀]]後半と推定されるが埋葬者は不明。[[欽明天皇]]と[[蘇我堅塩媛|堅塩媛]]の陵墓であるとの説もある。 古くは「五条野丸山古墳」と呼称されていた。[[明治時代]]になり「見瀬丸山古墳」の名称で呼ばれるようになったが、地元においては後円部は五条野町、前方部は大軽町が大部分を占めているため、「見瀬」を前置する呼称は適当でないとして現在でも丸山古墳と称されている。 == 基礎データ == 古墳は丘陵上の傾斜地に設けられている。極めて大規模な前方後円墳であり、全長は318メートル、前方部高さ15メートル、幅210メートル、後円部の径155メートル、高さ21メートル、前方部の幅210メートルにおよぶ。これは[[渋谷向山古墳|景行天皇陵]]を上回って奈良県下では最大、日本全国においても[[河内大塚山古墳]]に次いで 6位の規模であり、[[古墳時代]]後期後半に築造されたものの中では最大の規模を誇っている。 また、[[横穴式石室]]の全長は28.4メートルで、全国第1位の規模である。[[羨道]]は一枚の長さ4.8メートルの巨大な自然石6枚で天井を覆い、長さ20.1メートル、幅1メートル以上、高さ1.5メートルほど。[[玄室]]の長さ8.3メートル、最大幅4.1メートル、高さ4.5メートルある。内部には刳抜式[[家形石棺]]が2基、L字型に直交するように置かれている。玄室内には約1メートルの土砂が堆積しており、石棺の身については詳細は不明だが、奥棺は蓋の長さが2.42メートル、幅1.44メートル、高さ0.42メートル。前棺は蓋の長さが2.75メートル、幅1.41メートル、高さ0.63メートル。材質は[[流紋岩]]質[[溶結凝灰岩]]で[[加古川]]付近の[[竜山石]]。 通常石室は円墳の中央に置かれるが、丸山古墳では中央から20mほどずれている。石室を作る際の石が大型化する中、かつ横穴式を目指したことで、中央まで横穴を掘れなかったのではないかとする説もある。 File:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara), zenkei-1.jpg|墳丘全景（東側） File:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara), zenkei-2.jpg|墳丘全景（西側） File:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara), haisho.jpg|後円部の畝傍陵墓参考地入り口 File:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara), kouenbu.jpg|後円部墳丘 File:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara), kouenbu-1.jpg|後円部（前方部から望む） File:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara), zenpoubu.jpg|前方部（後円部から望む、背景は[[畝傍山]]） == 研究史 == [[image:Misemaruyamakohun_Seisekizushi.jpg|thumb|right|250px|『聖蹟図志』中の図を一部抜粋
左下が丸山古墳、右上は石室および石棺の詳細]] [[画像:480220 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|right|250px|上空から見た丸山古墳（1948年当時）。{{国土航空写真}}]] [[画像:mise_maru_stereo.jpg|thumb|350px|丸山古墳の[[ステレオグラム|ステレオ]]空中写真 {{国土航空写真}}]] 埋葬者が誰であるかは近世の江戸時代中期ころから論争があった。[[天武天皇]]と[[持統天皇]]の合同陵としては、丸山古墳とする説と、南西に1kmほど離れた場所にある[[野口王墓]]とする説とにわかれていた。 [[江戸時代]]後期から[[明治時代]]にかけて幾度か石室内部の調査が行われている。[[寛政]]8年([[1796年]])『惟徳実見記』で[[堤惟徳]]（つつみこれのり）は水が腰に及ぶ中、玄室の長さを3[[丈]]、幅1丈半と計測した。[[嘉永]]元年([[1848年]])の[[北浦定政]]『打墨縄(うつすみなわ)』にも記録がある。嘉永7年([[1854年]])、津久井清影『聖蹟図志』によれば、石室内には2つの家形石棺が残されており、入り口側は石室右に寄って主軸と平行に、奥側は横を向いておかれている。羨道は段々奥低く水溜れり、天井石六枚、長さ14[[間]]。玄室は天井石三枚で長さ4間、幅3間。と記されている。[[安政]]2年([[1855年]])には[[脇坂安宅]]が羨道の長さを「奥行11間3[[尺]]4[[寸]]」、玄室は3間、幅と高さ2間半と計測している。 [[明治時代]]に入ると、造幣局鋳造技術伝授のお雇い外国人として来日し、趣味として古墳調査を行った[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]は「日本最大のドルメン（横穴式石室）」と評価した。記録によると羨道は巨大な自然石6枚（最大のものは16[[フィート]]）で天井を覆い、長さ約60フィート、高さ8～10フィート、幅4～8フィート、壁は巨大な粗い自然石を積んでいる。羨道を40フィート進んだところで、内部に4フィートほどの深さで水が溜まっており、玄室には進めなかったが、かろうじて水面に顔を出している二つの家形石棺様子を観察した。ゴーランド『日本のドルメンとその築造者』出土品は多くが大英博物館に保存されている。 これらいくつもの調査にもかかわらず、江戸時代末から明治にかけて行われた天皇陵比定作業では、天武・持統合同陵との治定と解除を何度か繰り返して確定しなかった。しかし、[[明治]]13年([[1880年]])、栂尾（現[[京都市]][[右京区]]）の高山寺から『[[阿不幾乃山陵記]]』（あふきのさんりょうき）という調書が発見され、その中に[[鎌倉時代]]に野口王墓古墳で盗掘があり、その古墳内の様子を詳細に綴っていたことから、宮内省によって二人の天皇の合同陵指定は[[野口王墓]]と治定され、丸山古墳は最終的には天皇陵指定を解かれ、後円部上段の一部が[[陵墓参考地]]に指定された。 指定解除後の調査で後円部より出土した[[唐式鏡]]が現在[[京都大学]]文学部博物館に所蔵されている。宮内庁の実測調査時に特徴から田辺編年のTK43型式の[[須恵器]]片が出土しており、5ないし6世紀半ばまでに築造された古墳に見られる[[埴輪]]は一つも発見されていないことからも、築造時期は6世紀後半であるとの説が有力視されている。350年間続いた巨大な前方後円墳時代から横穴式石室古墳への過渡期を示す重要な古墳である。 == 1991年の石室撮影写真を巡る議論 == [[平成]]3年([[1991年]])、橿原市在住の児童が友人と遊んでいた際、同古墳の柵外において横穴式石室羨道への入り口を発見した。数日前の大雨で古墳の土砂が崩れて穴が露出したものである。この話を聞いた児童の父親は、5月30日早朝の出勤前に自身の子と共に羨道を通って内部に入り、カメラで石室内部を撮影した。父親から連絡を受けた大阪の[[朝日放送テレビ|朝日放送]]が撮影した写真の解析を、[[東海大学]]情報技術センターと[[コニカ]]の共同作業によっておこなった。寸法については、撮影者の子供が写っている写真を基に解析し決定された。江戸時代の記録通りに配置されていた家形石棺はどちらも蓋近くまで泥で埋まっており、蓋に付く縄掛け突起の特長から、手前の石棺は刳抜式で6世紀の第3四半世紀に、奥の石棺は7世紀の第1四半世紀にそれぞれ造られたと推定された。[[花崗岩]]製の石室正面を巨石の重量は推定100トンを越え、[[石舞台古墳]]のそれの75トンをもしのぐ大きさと判明した。石室はその石積様式から6世紀末から7世紀初めに構築されたと考えられた。 その後、[[森浩一]][[同志社大学]]教授(当時)が12月10日の大阪講演でこの話を取り上げ、12月26日[[テレビ朝日]]の[[ニュースステーション]]の番組内においても撮影された30枚の写真が放送された。おそらくは本物の天皇陵内部を垣間見る機会を得た国民からは強い関心が寄せられた。古墳研究の専門家の間においてもこれらの写真の有する学術的価値は極めて高いと評価された。 その後、翌平成4年([[1992年]])8月10日から9月15日まで[[宮内庁書陵部]]による開口部の閉塞工事にあわせて簡単な実測調査が行われ、後日報告書が作成、公表された。 == その後の被葬者をめぐる論議 == 以前より地元ではこの[[檜前]]（ひのくま）の地に欽明天皇陵があるとの確証のない伝承があった。 『[[日本書紀]]』には欽明天皇は御代32年([[571年]])に崩御した後、[[河内]]古市（現・大阪府[[羽曳野市]]）での[[殯]]（もがり）の後に檜隈坂合陵（ひのくまさかあいりょう）に葬じ、欽明陵には[[推古天皇]]28年([[620年]])に葺石、盛土の記録が見える。現在欽明陵に比定されている[[梅山古墳]]は、丸山同様にかつての桧隈の地にあり、大規模な[[葺石]]も発見されているが、丸山古墳はこの形跡が見られない。 一方で、『日本書紀』推古紀に「推古天皇20年([[612年]])2月20日、皇太夫人[[蘇我堅塩媛|堅塩媛]]（きたしひめ）を檜隈大陵に改葬し、軽の巷（かるのちまた）に「[[誄|しのびこと]]」しのびことを「誅」と書くのは誤字。似ているが別の字である。たてまつる」と見える。この「軽の巷」は当時の[[下ツ道]]と[[阿倍山田道]]の交点で現在の丈六交差点にあたり、丸山古墳の北側に位置する。堅塩媛は欽明天皇后であり推古天皇の生母でもあることから、改葬の理由を夫婦合葬とすれば、やはり欽明陵であった可能性が出てくることになる。 この他、当時蘇我氏が付近を根拠地としていたことから、[[蘇我稲目]]の名も被葬者に挙げられている{{Cite news |title= 奈良・都塚古墳:まるで階段ピラミッド…専門家から驚き|newspaper=毎日新聞 |date=2014-08-13 |author= |url=http://mainichi.jp/select/news/20140814k0000m040045000c.html |accessdate=2014-08-13 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140814060357/http://mainichi.jp/select/news/20140814k0000m040045000c.html |archivedate=2014-08-13 }}[[斎藤忠]]『古墳文化と古代国家』至文堂、1966年。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 文献 == * 季刊考古学・別冊2 　『見瀬丸山古墳と天皇陵』　猪熊兼勝他　[[雄山閣]]　1992年　ISBN 4639010990 * 書陵部紀要　第45号 『畝傍陵墓参考地石室現況調査報告』　[[宮内庁書陵部]]　1994年 * 古代を考える 57 　『見瀬丸山古墳の検討』　古代を考える会　1996年 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2013|丸山古墳}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 北浦定政『打墨縄』]　奈良県立図書情報館　電子資料 {{DEFAULTSORT:まるやまこふん かしはらし}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:欽明天皇]] [[Category:天武天皇]]

[[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial reference sites]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]]

This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. (Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial reference sites]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]]

{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial reference sites]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]]

The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. revision.text:
[[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library (Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies
