Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. revision.text:
[[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:qq1009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * revision.text:
[[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).

This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara revision.comment: Text replacement - "|須恵器}}" to "|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | Alternate name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Kirikubishi type) | Dimensions = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | Construction era = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | Excavated items = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial reference sites]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == Dimensions and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Imperial Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara revision.comment: another mass edit to try to fix parameters
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | alternate_name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Kirikubishi type) | area = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial reference sites]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-shape. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | alternate_name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Kirikubishi type) | area = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | Notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳}}, {{ill|Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}. The entire site, excluding the designated portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These dimensions surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == Notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial reference sites]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]]
