So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | alternate_name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (Kirikubishi type) | area = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kitashihime|qq=}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic Site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|qq=}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|qq=}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kako River (Japan)|qq=}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kitashihime|qq=}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Archives and Mausolea Department]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[sn:aruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]]

(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kitashihime|qq=}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic Site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|qq=}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|qq=}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kako River (Japan)|qq=}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kitashihime|qq=}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Archives and Mausolea Department]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[sn:aruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | alternate_name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (Kirikubishi type) | area = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}
(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kitashihime|qq=}} | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic Site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|qq=}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|qq=}} in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a large {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the {{ill|Kako River (Japan)|lt=Tatsuyama area|ja|加古川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kako River (Japan)|qq=}}. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}} or as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} and {{ill|Soga no Kitashihime|ja|蘇我堅塩媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Kitashihime|qq=}}. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' {{ill|Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|ja|宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency Shoryobu|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=}}, 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Archives and Mausolea Department]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[sn:aruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]]

{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|qq=}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|qq=}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Archives and Mausolea Department]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[sn:aruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | alternate_name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Kirikubishi type) | area = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]
(Hypothetical) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]] | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic Site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|qq=}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|qq=}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Archives and Mausolea Department]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[sn:aruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]]

The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Archives and Mausolea Department]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[sn:aruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | alternate_name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Kirikubishi type) | area = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]
(Hypothetical) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]] | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic Site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|qq=}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|qq=}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Archives and Mausolea Department]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[sn:aruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:欽明天皇]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | alternate_name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Kirikubishi type) | area = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]
(Hypothetical) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]] | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic Site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|qq=}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|qq=}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Archives and Mausolea Department]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[sn:aruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:欽明天皇]] [[Category:Candidate Imperial Mausoleums]]

The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Archives and Mausolea Department]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[sn:aruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:欽明天皇]] [[Category:Candidate Imperial Mausoleums]]

Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|qq=}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|qq=}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | alternate_name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|33.12|N|135|47|53.98|E|region:JP-29}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Kirikubishi type) | area = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]
(Hypothetical) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]] | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic Site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | image = [[File:111009 Maruyama Kofun aerial.jpg|280px]]
{{National Land Aerial Photography}} | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|qq=}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|qq=}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Archives and Mausolea Department]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[sn:aruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:欽明天皇]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]
