Height: 21m | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]
(Hypothetical) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]] | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic Site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | image = File:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara), zenkei-1.jpg | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)|1=Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630214}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11630214}}

Height: 21m | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]
(Hypothetical) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]] | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic Site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | image = File:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara), zenkei-1.jpg | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)|1=Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630214}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11630214}}

(Hypothetical) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]] | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic Site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | image = File:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara), zenkei-1.jpg | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)|1=Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630214}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11630214}}

Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)|1=Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630214}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11630214}}

Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)|1=Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630214}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11630214}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11630214}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)|1=Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630214}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11630214}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]][[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]][[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11630214]] Wikidata name: Maruyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | alternate_name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.475867|N|135.798328|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Kirikubishi type) | area = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]
(Hypothetical) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]] | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic Site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | image = File:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara), zenkei-1.jpg | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)|1=Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630214}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11630214}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11630214}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]][[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]][[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]][[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]][[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11630214]] Wikidata name: Maruyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | alternate_name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.475867|N|135.798328|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Kirikubishi type) | area = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]
(Hypothetical) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]] | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic Site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | image = File:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara), zenkei-1.jpg | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)|1=Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630214}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11630214}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)|1=Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630214}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Maruyama Kofun | alternate_name = Gojōno Maruyama Kofun / Ogaru Maruyama Kofun / Mise Maruyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.475867|N|135.798328|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Kirikubishi type) | area = Total length: 318m
Height: 21m | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]
(Hypothetical) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]] | Mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area" | Historic Site = National Historic Site "Maruyama Kofun" | excavations = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | image = File:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara), zenkei-1.jpg | notes = Sixth largest in Japan, largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National Edition)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018).
Final keyhole-shaped tumulus for emperors | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Maruyama Kofun''' (丸山古墳), also known as the {{ill|Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja|五条野丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)|1=Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630214}}, {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|lt=Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja|大軽丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru Maruyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, or {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, is located in the towns of {{ill|Mise, Kashihara|ja|見瀬町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Gojōno, Kashihara|ja|五条野町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōno, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ogaru, Kashihara|ja|大軽町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogaru, Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], among the largest in Japan. The actual interred individuals are unknown, but the rear mound is designation1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]. The entire site, excluding the designation1 portion, is protected as a National Historic Site under the name "Maruyama Kofun." At 318 meters in length, it is the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture and Ranks sixth nationally. It is also the final keyhole-shaped tumulus attributed to an emperor. == area and Features == The tumulus is situated on a hillside and is an exceptionally large keyhole-shaped tumulus. It measures 318m in length, with the rear mound having a diameter of 155m and a height of 21m. The front section measures 210m in width and 15m in height. These area surpass those of the {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Emperor Keikō) and place it as the sixth-largest tumulus in Japan. The {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} extends 28.4m, making it the longest in Japan. The corridor (羨道) is covered by six massive natural stones, the largest of which measures 4.8m. The chamber measures 20.1m in length, over 1m in width, and 1.5m in height. The burial chamber (玄室) is 8.3m long, 4.1m wide, and 4.5m high. Inside are two carved house-shaped stone sarcophagi (刳抜式家形石棺), positioned in an L-type. The materials are rhyolitic welded tuff (竜山石) sourced from the [[:en:Kako River (Japan)|Tatsuyama area]]. == Research and Historical Debate == Maruyama Kofun has been the subject of debate since the Edo period regarding its interred individuals. It has been alternately proposed as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] or as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and [[:en:Soga no Kitashihime|Soga no Kitashihime]]. The tumulus was investigated multiple times during the Edo and Meiji periods. In 1880, documents from the Kamakura period revealed details of a robbery at the nearby {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}}. This led the Imperial Household Agency to designate the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Tomb|qq=}} as the joint tomb of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō, while Maruyama Kofun was reclassified as a reference area. == notes and References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Quarterly Archaeology Special Issue 2: ''Mise Maruyama Kofun and Mausoleums,'' Kenkatsu Inokuma, Yuuzankaku, 1992. ISBN 4639010990 * ''Records of Unebi Mausoleum Reference Area Stone Chamber Survey,'' [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], 1994. * ''Mise Maruyama Kofun Studies,'' Ancient Studies Group, 1996. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2013|Maruyama Kofun}} * [http://opacsvr01.library.pref.nara.jp/mylimedio/dl/page.do?bookid=743268&tocid=0&page=13&pageseq=13 Kitaura Sadamasa's "Utsusu Minawa"] - Nara Prefectural Library {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|丸山古墳 (橿原市)|version=103362375|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:丸山古墳_(橿原市)]] [[en:Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)]] [[ja:丸山古墳]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{Commons category|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}} [[ja:丸山古墳 (橿原市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11630214}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
